100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 19, 1926, the British Empire faced escalating tensions in India as Mahatma Gandhi's civil disobedience movement gained traction, prompting the British government to consider stricter measures. In the U.S., the Senate moved forward with discussions on the controversial McNary-Haugen Bill aimed at supporting farmers amid growing agricultural distress. Meanwhile, in Germany, the political climate remained volatile as economic challenges continued to plague the Weimar Republic.
